mxCell.value:
MCP Client / AI Agent
(Python Lib or Container)

Amazon Bedrock
Agent + Guardrails + KB
App Inference Profiles
(per-tenant cost tracking)
Security and Observability: CloudWatch | GuardDuty | Security Hub | CloudTrail | KMS | IAM | Macie | Config | Inspector
SaaS Network Monitoring : Detailed Multi-Tenant Architecture

Customer A
Keysight App + MCP Client
UC1: .txt | UC2: CSV/TXT/PCAP

Customer B
Keysight App + MCP Client
UC1: .txt | UC2: CSV/TXT/PCAP
TIER 3 : Isolated (Silo) - High - Dedicated bucket + KMS CMK per tenant

Agent reads tenant_id from
session to route to correct tier
Security / Observability: CloudWatch | GuardDuty | Security Hub | CloudTrail | KMS (per-tenant CMK) | IAM | Macie | Config | Inspector | VPC Endpoints (Bedrock,S3)

App Inference Profiles
1 profile per tenant
Tagged: tenant_id=X
Usage auto-attributed

AWS Cost Explorer
Filter by tenant_id tag
Per-tenant cost views

Cost and Usage
Reports (CUR)
S3 export, Athena query
Granular line items

Auth Flow (No API Keys, No Lambda Authorizer):
(1) Engineer authenticates with Cognito (through app and MCP client)
(2) Cognito returns JWT with tenant_id claim
(3) MCP sends request + JWT in Authorization header
(4) API GW Cognito Authorizer validates JWT natively
(5) Forwards to downstream Lambda with tenant context
(6) Downstream Lambda loads relevant inference profile from Dynamo DB for subsequent Bedrock API calls

AUTHENTICATION AND AUTHORIZATION
- Amazon Cognito User Pool with Grant Tenant_Id
- Each tenant gets a dedicated App Client with tenant_id custom claim
- API Gateway uses built-in Cognito Authorizer 
- JWT token carries tenant_id, scopes, and expiry
- Token refresh handled by MCP client SDK automatically
- No API keys required; JWT is the single auth mechanism

MULTI-TENANCY STRATEGY (3-Tier S3 Vector Isolation)
- Tier 1 (Pool): Shared S3 bucket for common knowledge base
Vendor documentation, product catalogs, best practices
All tenants have read-only access; lowest cost
- Tier 2 (Bridge): Single S3 bucket with prefix-per-tenant
Operational tickets, service history, incident patterns
IAM condition keys enforce s3:prefix matching tenant_id
Bedrock KB metadata filtering ensures query isolation
- Tier 3 (Silo): Dedicated S3 bucket per tenant
PII, proprietary network configs, credentials, topology data
Per-tenant KMS CMK for encryption at rest
Separate IAM role per bucket; S3 Object Lock enabled
VPC Gateway Endpoint ensures no public internet transit

BEDROCK AGENT ORCHESTRATION
- Agent receives tenant_id via session attributes from Lambda along with inference profile attached
- Action Group 1: Retrieves from shared KB (all tenants)
- Action Group 2: Retrieves from tenant prefix (filtered by tenant_id)
- Action Group 3: Retrieves from dedicated silo bucket (isolated)
- Agent decides which tiers to query based on request type
- UC1 (Script Gen): Primarily Tier 1 + Tier 2
- UC2 (Incident RCA): All tiers + Salesforce ticket data
- Guardrails applied before every LLM invocation

COST MANAGEMENT: APPLICATION INFERENCE PROFILES
- One App Inference Profile created per tenant
- Each profile tagged with tenant_id cost allocation tag
- Lambda uses profile ARN (not model ID) in InvokeModel calls
- Tags auto-flow into AWS billing records per request
- Enables: per-tenant cost attribution without custom metering

COST VISIBILITY AND REPORTING
- AWS Cost Explorer: Filter by tenant_id tag for real-time views
- Cost and Usage Reports (CUR): Exported to S3, queryable via Athena
Granular line-item detail per tenant per model per day
- CloudWatch Custom Metrics: Tokens/min, cost/5min per profile
- Multi-level alarms (Green/Yellow/Red) per tenant usage
- SNS alerts when threshold breached (cost or token rate)
- Per-tenant CloudWatch dashboards for SLA monitoring
- Eliminates need for custom DynamoDB token-counting logic

SECURITY AND COMPLIANCE
- AWS Shield Standard: DDoS protection (free tier)
- AWS WAF: Rate limiting, geo-blocking, SQL injection rules
- Bedrock Guardrails: PII redaction, content filtering, denied topics
- Amazon Macie: Automated PII discovery on S3 vector buckets
- GuardDuty: Threat detection across all accounts
- Security Hub: Centralized compliance findings
- CloudTrail: Full API audit trail for all services
- Per-tenant KMS CMKs: Encryption isolation at rest
- VPC Endpoints: S3 Gateway + Bedrock PrivateLink
- IAM least-privilege roles per Lambda function

ASYNC AND RESPONSE PATTERNS
- Synchronous: Direct API GW response for small payloads
- Asynchronous: WebSocket connection for long-running RCA
- DynamoDB stores job status (PENDING/PROCESSING/COMPLETE)
- Client polls or receives WebSocket push on completion

DATA INGESTION PIPELINE
- Salesforce: AppFlow scheduled sync per tenant (daily/hourly)
- Jira: AppFlow connector syncs tickets/issues per tenant
- Both feed into Embed Lambda for vectorization
- Lambda classifies data sensitivity and routes to correct S3 tier
- Titan Embeddings V2/ Cohere generates vectors stored in S3
- Bedrock KB data source sync triggered on new data arrival
- UC2 data (CSV/TXT/PCAP) parsed and normalized by Lambda
- File format detection and schema validation before embedding

SCALABILITY AND OPERATIONS
- Fully serverless: no EC2, no cluster management
- API GW: 10K RPS default, increase via quota request
- Lambda: Concurrent executions scale automatically
- Bedrock: Provisioned throughput for predictable latency
- S3: Unlimited storage, 5500 GET/s per prefix
- Adding new tenant: Cognito client + Inference Profile + S3

DISASTER RECOVERY AND RELIABILITY
- S3: 11 nines durability, cross-region replication optional
- DynamoDB: Point-in-time recovery enabled
- Lambda: Multi-AZ by default
- API GW: Regional endpoint 
- Cognito: Multi-AZ, no single point of failure
- (Optional) Dead Letter Queue (SQS) for failed Lambda invocations

MONITORING AND ALERTING
- CloudWatch Logs: All Lambda execution logs
- X-Ray: End-to-end distributed tracing
- Custom metrics: Latency per tenant, error rates
- Graduated alarms: Green (OK) / Yellow (warning) / Red (critical)
- SNS notifications on cost/token threshold breach
- S3 Access Logs + Athena for vector access auditing

FUTURE CONSIDERATIONS
- Model routing: Different models per use case / cost tier
- Fine-tuning: Custom models per tenant if needed
- Caching: ElastiCache for frequent KB queries
- Multi-region (Geo based SaaS) for serving global customers
- Tenant onboarding automation via Control plane (e.g. Step Functions)
